humanities_role_explanation: The paper focuses on translating classical Arabic Islamic literature (Qur'an, Hadith, Tafseer). The translation pipeline is tailored specifically to preserve sacred text accuracy using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and domain-specific prompt constraints, though the ultimate validation relies on generic semantic vector similarity.
benchmark_modalities_explanation: While the overall system utilizes multimodal models (GPT-4o) for OCR on document images and supports optional text-to-speech audio generation, the core translation and back-translation evaluation are executed and analyzed purely on text.
analytical_tasks_explanation: The primary task is the translation of classical, highly figurative, and theological Arabic prose into English. The quality of this translation is analyzed using an automated back-translation task followed by semantic similarity calculation, testing whether the LLM can preserve complex historical and religious meanings across languages.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: The paper outlines a localized application pipeline and its translation validation protocol rather than a general-purpose evaluation benchmark.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluates proprietary LLMs including OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini and Anthropic's Claude 3 Haiku, with preliminary testing of open-source models like Llama, Qwen, and Silma.

llm_is_primary_system.note: LLMs serve as the primary systems for translation, OCR processing, and back-translation validation.

arts_humanities_data.note: The target corpus is the Al-Shamela Library, representing historical Islamic literature, jurisprudence, and theological scriptures.

quantitative_metrics.note: Evaluates translation fidelity using vector embedding cosine similarity scores.

english_language.note: The paper is written in English.

summary: The paper introduces an automated translation pipeline designed to translate classical Islamic literature from the Al-Shamela library into English using LLMs. It addresses domain-specific challenges such as Arabic OCR diacritics and script accuracy preservation (using RAG), and implements a validation framework based on back-translation and embedding-based similarity.
key_findings: GPT-4o-mini was chosen as the most cost-effective and high-quality forward translation model. Automated validation demonstrated that 71% of OpenAI back-translations and 86% of Anthropic back-translations achieved an acceptable semantic cosine similarity score of >= 0.7 compared to the original Arabic source.
